We recommend that Management directs the Municipal Health Office in
collaboration with the Accounting Department to:
a. Immediately craft a well-defined guidelines through an internal policy, on the
Request for Procurement, Receipt, Disposition and Monitoring of the Medicines
and Medical supplies/equipment to ensure that accountabilities and safeguarding,
including risk ownership are in place while in its custody.
b. The written guidelines may include, among others:
b.1.Request for Medicines:
1. Barangay Health Profiling should be done annually to all 28 barangays;
2. This in turn should be submitted to the Public Health Nurse (PHN) for the
preparation of the LGU Consolidated Report on Barangay Health Profile;
3. The Consolidated Report on Barangay Health Profile should be reconciled with
the Medicines and Medical Supplies/equipment Inventory Report
4. The result of the reconciliation will now serve as the basis for the preparation for
the request of stocks to avoid shortage of medicines/medical supplies/equipment.
5. A Purchase Request together with the PNDF required certification should be
prepared by the MHO and forwarded to the LCE for the approval and eventual
procurement
b.2. Receipt of Medicines:
1. All receipt of medicines and medical supplies/equipment should be thoroughly
inspected by the Custodian and an MHO representative simultaneously using the
Delivery Receipt and Purchase Request as their basis. Take note of the Quantity
of Medicines and the Expiration dates;
2. The Medicines received should be posted in the logbook inventory. A separate set
of logbook should be utilized for stocks from different sources (LGU/
DOH/Province)
3. Ensure that these are properly stocked
4. In cases where the custodian is not available, there should be a medicine
custodian-alternate, designated by the MHO, and a proper turn-over should be
made.
5. There should be a registry for medicines for all receipts and issuances to ensure
proper tracking of the medicines and /medical supplies/equipment
b.3. Issuances of Medicines
1. A copy of the medical receipts/request for release from the RHU/Mayor should be
evidenced every time there is an issuance of medicines and medical
supplies/equipment
2. It should be attached to the Authorized MHO Medicine slip and signed by the
custodian and recipient
3. It should be filed in a designated folder for Medicines Issue Slip for easy
reference
4. The recipient should sign in the logbook upon release of the medicines
5. A monthly Report of issuances should be prepared and these should be reconciled
with the stocks available
b.4. Monitoring:
1. Conduct of Physical Inventory/ Inspection Report at least twice a month
2. Post the List of Inventory in a conspicuous place of the MHO
The MHO, should strictly ensure the proper implementation of the policy. Non-
compliance will be his sole responsibility/accountability, being the head of the
department.

observations.managementAction: Management was required to strictly comply with the guidelines set under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-001 and to coordinate on budget reviews.
observations.reason: Reliance of the LGU on the budget review of the Sanguniang Panlalawigan as to who did not make comment on the said items on the budget; thus the objectives were not totally attained.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the lacking supporting documents as required under COA Circular 2012-001 and process payments only with complete and appropriate documents.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant were required to submit the missing supporting documents to COA and pay only claims with complete supporting documents.
observations.reason: There are still disbursements with incomplete supporting documents for CY 2016 and prior periods; difficulty in retrieval of documents.

observations.recommendation: Submit immediately the documents enumerated in the last paragraph to the Audit Team for verification. Strictly observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 2003-003.
observations.managementAction: Disbursing Officer for Confidential and Intelligence Fund was required to immediately submit the documents relevant to the said disbursements to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Submission of the required documents for CY 2015 were not yet submitted to the Audit Team as of report date.

observations.recommendation: Fully implement the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 and coordinate with the Provincial Solid Waste Management Board for the programs and projects to be undertaken.
observations.managementAction: Management committed to revise the Solid Waste Management Plan and coordinate with Provincial Solid Waste Management Board and to intensify information drive and to seek MOA with a company for waste management facility.
observations.reason: Unavailability of sufficient resources and the SWM plan and closure/rehabilitation actions were still in process; hence partial implementation.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Revenue Collection Clerk to render daily collections to the Municipal Treasurer and instruct the Treasurer to verify the Statement of Collections against accountable forms and ensure collections are reported and remitted; comply with NGAS requirements.
observations.managementAction: Revenue Collection Clerk, through the Treasurer, was required to immediately remit the daily collections to the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Treasurer to verify collections against accountable forms as prescribed; monitoring was advised.
observations.reason: Lack of strict and consistent monitoring by the Municipal Treasurer; some RCCs did not render reports and remittances regularly.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the Supply Officer to comply strictly with the provisions on trip tickets, driver’s trip tickets, monthly report of official travels and Monthly Report of Fuel/Oil Consumption and prepare required reports to support payment of gasoline/fuel and lubricants monthly.
observations.managementAction: Supply Officer to comply with provisions of PD 1445 and COA Circular 77-61; prepare trip tickets in serially numbered forms and distribute copies; prepare Summary of Requisition and Issue Voucher and Monthly Report of Official Travel and Monthly Report of Fuel/Oil Consumption.
observations.reason: There were still missing trip tickets and other attachments and incomplete fill-up of trip tickets resulting in difficulty in ascertaining consumption.

observations.recommendation: Ensure that the Physician-in-Charge or Procurement Officer determines that the drug products being requisitioned are consistent with the Philippine National Drug Formulary and attach the required certification for every Purchase Request and maintain stock cards and prepare required supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: MHO was required to attach the required certification for every Purchase Request and the General Services Officer/Property Officer and Treasurer to attach all required documents to support the validity of the procured medicines and to maintain stock cards consistent with NGAS requirements.
observations.reason: Lack of competent personnel and incomplete preparation of required certifications and stock cards; some documents were verified but some were not prepared.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the Supply Officer to strictly observe COA Circular No. 97-002 and require concerned officers and employees to return/refund unexpended balances of cash advances to the government and collect outstanding balances.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant was required to strictly observe COA Circular No. 97-002 and to collect the unrefunded balances and process refunds through demand letters and appropriate collection procedures.
observations.reason: Difficulty in retrieving unexpended balances from separated employees and some balances remained outstanding as of report date.
